# Exodus Chapter 40 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 112–113; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 83–84 | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_040_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_040_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_040_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_040_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

## YHWH Commands Moses to Erect the Tabernacle (vv. 1–8)

**1** And YHWH [**YHWH**] spoke [**vayedabber**] to [**el**] Moses [**Mosheh**], saying [**lemor**]:

**2** "On the day of [**beyom**] the first [**ha'echad**] month [**hachodesh**], on the first [**be'echad**] of the month [**lachodesh**], you shall erect [**taqim**] the tabernacle [**et-mishkan**] of the tent of [**ohel**] meeting [**mo'ed**].

**3** And you shall place [**vesamta**] there [**sham**] the ark of [**et-aron**] the testimony [**ha'edut**], and you shall screen [**vesakota**] over [**al**] the ark [**ha'aron**] with the veil [**et-happarokhet**].

**4** And you shall bring in [**veheve'ta**] the table [**et-hashulchan**] and arrange [**ve'arakhta**] its arrangement [**et-erkho**]; and you shall bring in [**veheve'ta**] the lampstand [**et-hammenorah**] and set up [**veha'aleta**] its lamps [**et-neroteyha**].

**5** And you shall set [**venatattah**] the altar of [**et-mizbach**] gold [**hazzahav**] for incense [**liqqtoret**] before [**lifney**] the ark of [**aron**] the testimony [**ha'edut**], and you shall put [**vesamta**] the screen of [**et-masakh**] the entrance [**happetach**] for the tabernacle [**lammishkan**].

**6** And you shall set [**venatattah**] the altar of [**et-mizbach**] the burnt offering [**ha'olah**] before [**lifney**] the entrance of [**petach**] the tabernacle [**mishkan**] of the tent of [**ohel**] meeting [**mo'ed**].

**7** And you shall set [**venatattah**] the basin [**et-hakkiyyor**] between [**beyn**] the tent of [**ohel**] meeting [**mo'ed**] and between [**uveyn**] the altar [**hammizbe'ach**], and you shall put [**venatattah**] water [**mayim**] there [**sham**].

**8** And you shall set up [**vesamta**] the court [**et-hechatser**] all around [**saviv**], and you shall put [**venatattah**] the screen of [**et-masakh**] the gate of [**sha'ar**] the court [**hechatser**].

---

## The Anointing (vv. 9–11)

**9** And you shall take [**velaqachta**] the anointing [**hammishchah**] oil [**et-shemen**] and anoint [**umashachta**] the tabernacle [**et-hammishkan**] and all [**ve'et-kol**] that [**asher**] is in it [**bo**], and you shall consecrate [**veqiddashta**] it [**oto**] and all [**ve'et-kol**] its vessels [**kelav**]; and it shall be [**vehayah**] holy [**qodesh**].

**10** And you shall anoint [**umashachta**] the altar of [**et-mizbach**] the burnt offering [**ha'olah**] and all [**ve'et-kol**] its vessels [**kelav**], and you shall consecrate [**veqiddashta**] the altar [**et-hammizbe'ach**]; and the altar [**hammizbe'ach**] shall be [**vehayah**] most holy [**qodesh qodashim**].

**11** And you shall anoint [**umashachta**] the basin [**et-hakkiyyor**] and [**ve'et**] its stand [**kanno**], and you shall consecrate [**veqiddashta**] it [**oto**].

---

## The Priestly Consecration (vv. 12–15)

**12** And you shall bring near [**vehiqravta**] Aaron [**et-Aharon**] and his sons [**ve'et-banav**] to [**el**] the entrance of [**petach**] the tent of [**ohel**] meeting [**mo'ed**], and you shall wash [**verachatsata**] them [**otam**] with water [**bammayim**].

**13** And you shall clothe [**vehilbashta**] Aaron [**et-Aharon**] with the holy [**haqqodesh**] garments [**et-bigdey**], and you shall anoint [**umashachta**] him [**oto**] and consecrate [**veqiddashta**] him [**oto**], and he shall minister as priest [**vekhihen**] to me [**li**].

**14** And his sons [**ve'et-banav**] you shall bring near [**taqriv**], and you shall clothe [**vehilbashta**] them [**otam**] with tunics [**kuttonot**].

**15** And you shall anoint [**umashachta**] them [**otam**] as [**ka'asher**] you anointed [**mashachta**] their father [**et-avihem**], and they shall minister as priests [**vekhihanu**] to me [**li**]; and it shall be [**vehayetah**] that their anointing [**mishchatam**] shall be [**lihyot**] for them [**lahem**] an everlasting [**olam**] priesthood [**lekehunnat**] throughout their generations [**ledorotam**]."

---

## Moses Erects the Tabernacle (vv. 16–19)

**16** And Moses [**Mosheh**] did [**vayya'as**] according to all [**kekhol**] that [**asher**] YHWH [**YHWH**] commanded [**tsivvah**] him [**oto**]; so [**ken**] he did [**asah**].

**17** And it was [**vayehi**] in the first [**harishon**] month [**bachodesh**], in the second [**hashenit**] year [**bashanah**], on the first [**be'echad**] of the month [**lachodesh**], the tabernacle [**hammishkan**] was erected [**huqam**].

**18** And Moses [**Mosheh**] erected [**vayyaqem**] the tabernacle [**et-hammishkan**] and he set [**vayyitten**] its bases [**et-adanav**] and he placed [**vayyasem**] its frames [**et-qerasahv**] and he put in [**vayyitten**] its bars [**et-berichav**] and he erected [**vayyaqem**] its pillars [**et-ammudav**].

**19** And he spread [**vayyifros**] the tent [**et-ha'ohel**] over [**al**] the tabernacle [**hammishkan**] and he placed [**vayyasem**] the covering of [**mikhseh**] the tent [**ha'ohel**] over it [**alav**] from above [**milma'lah**], as [**ka'asher**] YHWH [**YHWH**] commanded [**tsivvah**] Moses [**et-Mosheh**].

---

## The Ark and the Veil (vv. 20–21)

**20** And he took [**vayyiqqach**] and placed [**vayyitten**] the testimony [**et-ha'edut**] into [**el**] the ark [**ha'aron**], and he set [**vayyasem**] the poles [**et-habbaddim**] on [**al**] the ark [**ha'aron**], and he put [**vayyitten**] the mercy seat [**et-hakkapporet**] on [**al**] the ark [**ha'aron**] from above [**milma'lah**].

**21** And he brought [**vayyave**] the ark [**et-ha'aron**] into [**el**] the tabernacle [**hammishkan**] and he set up [**vayyasem**] the veil [**et-parokhet**] of the screen [**hammasakh**] and he screened over [**vayyasekh al**] the ark of [**aron**] the testimony [**ha'edut**], as [**ka'asher**] YHWH [**YHWH**] commanded [**tsivvah**] Moses [**et-Mosheh**].

---

## The Table and the Lampstand (vv. 22–25)

**22** And he placed [**vayyitten**] the table [**et-hashulchan**] in the tent of [**be'ohel**] meeting [**mo'ed**], on [**al**] the side of [**yerekh**] the tabernacle [**hammishkan**] northward [**tsafonah**], outside [**michutz**] the veil [**lapparokhet**].

**23** And he arranged [**vayya'arokh**] upon it [**alav**] an arrangement of [**erekh**] bread [**lechem**] before [**lifney**] YHWH [**YHWH**], as [**ka'asher**] YHWH [**YHWH**] commanded [**tsivvah**] Moses [**et-Mosheh**].

**24** And he placed [**vayyasem**] the lampstand [**et-hammenorah**] in the tent of [**be'ohel**] meeting [**mo'ed**], opposite [**nokhach**] the table [**hashulchan**], on [**al**] the side of [**yerekh**] the tabernacle [**hammishkan**] southward [**negbah**].

**25** And he set up [**vayya'al**] the lamps [**hannerot**] before [**lifney**] YHWH [**YHWH**], as [**ka'asher**] YHWH [**YHWH**] commanded [**tsivvah**] Moses [**et-Mosheh**].

---

## The Golden Altar and the Screen (vv. 26–28)

**26** And he placed [**vayyasem**] the altar of [**mizbach**] gold [**hazzahav**] in the tent of [**be'ohel**] meeting [**mo'ed**] before [**lifney**] the veil [**happarokhet**].

**27** And he burned [**vayyaqter**] on it [**alav**] fragrant [**samim**] incense [**qetoret**], as [**ka'asher**] YHWH [**YHWH**] commanded [**tsivvah**] Moses [**et-Mosheh**].

**28** And he set up [**vayyasem**] the screen of [**et-masakh**] the entrance [**happetach**] for the tabernacle [**lammishkan**].

---

## The Altar of Burnt Offering and the Basin (vv. 29–32)

**29** And the altar of [**et-mizbach**] the burnt offering [**ha'olah**] he placed [**sam**] at the entrance of [**petach**] the tabernacle [**mishkan**] of the tent of [**ohel**] meeting [**mo'ed**], and he offered up [**vayya'al**] upon it [**alav**] the burnt offering [**et-ha'olah**] and the grain offering [**veha'et-hamminchah**], as [**ka'asher**] YHWH [**YHWH**] commanded [**tsivvah**] Moses [**et-Mosheh**].

**30** And he placed [**vayyasem**] the basin [**et-hakkiyyor**] between [**beyn**] the tent of [**ohel**] meeting [**mo'ed**] and between [**uveyn**] the altar [**hammizbe'ach**], and he put [**vayyitten**] water [**mayim**] there [**shammah**] for washing [**lerachtsah**].

**31** And Moses [**Mosheh**] and Aaron [**ve'Aharon**] and his sons [**uvanav**] washed [**verachatsu**] from it [**mimmennu**] their hands [**et-yedeyhem**] and their feet [**ve'et-ragleyhem**].

**32** When they went [**bevo'am**] into [**el**] the tent of [**ohel**] meeting [**mo'ed**] and when they drew near [**uvqorvatam**] to [**el**] the altar [**hammizbe'ach**] they washed [**yirchatsu**], as [**ka'asher**] YHWH [**YHWH**] commanded [**tsivvah**] Moses [**et-Mosheh**].

---

## The Court Erected (v. 33)

**33** And he erected [**vayyaqem**] the court [**et-hechatser**] all around [**saviv**] the tabernacle [**lammishkan**] and the altar [**velammizbe'ach**], and he put up [**vayyitten**] the screen of [**et-masakh**] the gate of [**sha'ar**] the court [**hechatser**]. And Moses [**Mosheh**] finished [**vayekhal**] the work [**et-hammelakhah**].

---

## The Glory of YHWH Fills the Tabernacle (vv. 34–38)

**34** And the cloud [**he'anan**] covered [**vayekhas**] the tent of [**et-ohel**] meeting [**mo'ed**], and the glory of [**ukhvod**] YHWH [**YHWH**] filled [**male**] the tabernacle [**et-hammishkan**].

**35** And Moses [**Mosheh**] was not able [**velo-yakhol**] to enter [**lavo**] into [**el**] the tent of [**ohel**] meeting [**mo'ed**], for [**ki**] the cloud [**he'anan**] dwelt [**shakhan**] upon it [**alav**], and the glory of [**ukhvod**] YHWH [**YHWH**] filled [**male**] the tabernacle [**et-hammishkan**].

**36** And when the cloud [**he'anan**] was taken up [**behe'alot**] from upon [**me'al**] the tabernacle [**hammishkan**], the sons of [**beney**] Israel [**Yisra'el**] would set out [**yis'u**] in all [**bekhol**] their journeys [**mas'eyhem**].

**37** And if [**ve'im**] the cloud [**he'anan**] was not [**lo**] taken up [**ye'aleh**], then they would not [**velo**] set out [**yis'u**] until [**ad**] the day of [**yom**] its being taken up [**he'aloto**].

**38** For [**ki**] the cloud of [**anan**] YHWH [**YHWH**] was upon [**al**] the tabernacle [**hammishkan**] by day [**yomam**], and fire [**ve'esh**] was [**tihyeh**] in it [**bo**] by night [**laylah**], before the eyes of [**le'eyney**] all [**khol**] the house of [**beyt**] Israel [**Yisra'el**] in all [**bekhol**] their journeys [**mas'eyhem**].

---

## Textual Notes

1. **v. 2 — taqim (תָּקִים)**: Hiphil imperfect 2ms of קום, "you shall cause to stand / erect." The Vaticanus reads στήσεις (steseis), confirming the causative sense.

2. **v. 4 — veha'aleta (וְהַעֲלֵיתָ)**: Hiphil perfect 2ms of עלה with waw-consecutive, literally "and you shall cause to go up [its lamps]," i.e., set up / kindle. LXX: ἐπιθήσεις (epitheseis), "you shall place upon."

3. **v. 10 — qodesh qodashim (קֹדֶשׁ קׇדָשִׁים)**: "Holy of holies" / "most holy." Preserved in formal doublet. LXX: ἅγιον τῶν ἁγίων (hagion ton hagion).

4. **v. 15 — lekehunnat olam ledorotam (לִכְהֻנַּת עוֹלָם לְדֹרֹתָם)**: "For an everlasting priesthood throughout their generations." The word order is preserved from the Hebrew construct chain.

5. **v. 17 — bashanah hashenit (בַּשָּׁנָה הַשֵּׁנִית)**: "In the second year" — i.e., the second year after the Exodus from Egypt. LXX adds τῇ ἐξόδῳ (te exodo) for clarity; MT leaves the reference implicit.

6. **v. 20 — et-ha'edut (אֶת־הָעֵדֻת)**: "The testimony" — referring to the two tablets of the covenant placed inside the ark. LXX: τὰ μαρτύρια (ta martyria).

7. **v. 29 — veha'et-hamminchah (וְאֶת־הַמִּנְחָה)**: "And the grain offering." The conjunction-and-direct-object-marker construction is preserved formally.

8. **v. 34 — male (מָלֵא)**: Qal perfect 3ms of מלא, "filled." The verb is stative, expressing the resultant state: the glory of YHWH filled and pervaded the tabernacle. LXX: ἐπλήσθη (eplesthe), aorist passive, "was filled."

9. **v. 35 — shakhan (שָׁכַן)**: Qal perfect 3ms of שׁכן, "dwelt / tabernacled." This is the verbal root from which mishkan (מִשְׁכָּן, "tabernacle / dwelling place") derives — a significant theological wordplay: the cloud "tabernacled" upon the tabernacle.

10. **vv. 36–37 — Conditional travel formula**: The cloud's movement governs Israel's journeying. The imperfect verbs (יִסְעוּ, יֵעָלֶה) express iterative/habitual action: "whenever the cloud was taken up... they would set out."

11. **v. 38 — le'eyney khol beyt Yisra'el (לְעֵינֵי כׇל־בֵּית יִשְׂרָאֵל)**: "Before the eyes of all the house of Israel." The closing formula emphasizes the visible, public nature of YHWH's presence. LXX reads πᾶς οἶκος Ισραηλ (pas oikos Israel).

12. **LXX Divergence — vv. 6–8**: The Vaticanus text follows a significantly different verse order in the instructions section compared to MT, rearranging certain elements of the courtyard and basin commands. The translation follows MT verse order throughout.

13. **LXX Plus — vv. 17ff**: Codex Vaticanus contains an extensive plus section after the erection narrative (corresponding roughly to MT 40:17–33), repeating material from chapters 36–39 regarding the construction of individual items. This represents the well-known LXX Exodus divergence in the tabernacle accounts. The formal translation follows the MT.
